title: Direct Messaging vs Broadcast
concept: Contrasting point-to-point addressing with public blackboard/channel communication for shared context.

exercises.prompt: What is a common format used for structured messages between agents?
exercises.explain: JSON and XML are popular formats for structured messages because they organize data clearly for communication between agents.

cards.title: Direct Messaging
cards.body: Direct messaging is like sending a private letter. It's a way for one agent to communicate with another without anyone else seeing.

cards.title: Broadcast Communication
cards.body: Broadcast communication is like an announcement made in class, where everyone can hear the message at the same time.

cards.title: Point-to-Point vs. Public Channel
cards.body: Think of point-to-point as a phone call between two friends, while broadcast is like a radio station playing music for everyone.

cards.title: Example of Direct Messaging
cards.body: A robot sends a direct message to another robot asking for its location. Only those two robots see the message.

cards.title: Myth: All Messaging is Public
cards.body: Not all messages are public! Direct messaging keeps conversations private, unlike broadcast messages.

cards.title: Visualizing Communication Types
cards.body: See how point-to-point connects just two agents, while broadcast reaches many at once.

exercises.prompt: What is the main difference between direct messaging and broadcast communication?

options.text: Direct messages are private, while broadcasts are public.
Direct messages must be short, while broadcasts can be long.
Direct messages can only be sent to many agents at once.
Direct messages need a password, while broadcasts do not.

exercises.explain: Direct messaging allows for private conversation between two agents, while broadcast communication shares messages publicly with many agents.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Direct messaging allows multiple agents to see the same message.
exercises.explain: Direct messaging is intended for two agents only, making it private and not visible to everyone.

exercises.prompt: Direct messaging is like sending a private ______, while broadcasting is like making an announcement.
exercises.sentence: Direct messaging is like sending a private letter, while broadcasting is like making an announcement.
exercises.explain: In this context, 'letter' illustrates the private and personal nature of direct messaging.

exercises.prompt: Which statement is an AI-generated response about communication types?

options.text: Broadcasting should always be used for urgent messages.
Direct messaging is less secure than broadcasting.
Direct messaging allows for conversations that are private and between specific agents.

exercises.explain: This statement accurately describes the nature of direct messaging as private and limited to specific agents.

exercises.prompt: In a scenario where Agent A needs to inform both Agent B and Agent C about a malfunction, what communication method should be used and why?

options.text: Direct messaging to Agent B only.
Direct messaging to both Agent B and Agent C.
Broadcasting the message to both Agents B and C.
Sending a private letter to both agents separately.

exercises.explain: Broadcasting the message is effective in this case because it allows both Agent B and Agent C to receive the information simultaneously without needing separate messages.
